externalProblemAnchor.problemStatement: Matbench public materials-property tasks provide externally inspectable property outcomes where descriptor-transfer claims must beat composition, formula-size, target-family, and split-leakage rivals before any hard-seed birth.
externalProblemAnchor.measuredTargetOutcome: cross-task materials property residual under descriptor-transfer perturbation after composition, formula-size, target-family, and shuffled-target controls
externalProblemAnchor.knownBaselineOrPrior: composition-only descriptors, formula size, element prevalence, target-family identity, split leakage, and shuffled-target controls are simple rivals for materials descriptor-transfer signals
externalProblemAnchor.externalValueRationale: A descriptor-transfer residual has external value only if it changes interpretation of public materials-property evaluation outcomes beyond ordinary composition and task-family artifacts.
externalProblemAnchor.domainScientificSignificance: A materials descriptor-transfer mechanism has domain scientific significance only when it identifies a reproducible property-outcome residual across public materials tasks that is not absorbed by composition, formula-size, target-family, split-leakage, or shuffled-target rivals.
externalProblemAnchor.discoveryScoredOutcome: Discovery-scored evidence would be a narrow computational-materials mechanism claim about public property outcome structure with independent holdout and replay support, not a matminer or pymatgen pipeline success.

gates.message: HardSeed birth requires a real external problem anchor before generator evidence can count.
External problem anchor must cite a public-safe external source.
External problem anchor must define a measured target outcome.
External problem anchor must define the known baseline or prior rival state.
External problem anchor must explain domain scientific significance beyond pipeline, package, or tool capability.
External problem anchor must define a discovery-scored outcome that is not only operational pipeline success.
External problem anchor must bind at least two public-safe significance evidence refs into the evidence set.
External problem anchor must be inspectable through the evidence refs.
Generator-only signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Package-only or pipeline-contract-only signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Internally interesting but externally unanchored signals are blocked before HardSeed birth.
Source-family documented behavior is blocked before HardSeed birth.
Known or trivial behavior is blocked before HardSeed birth.

domainSignificanceHypothesis.statement: A materials descriptor-transfer mechanism has domain scientific significance only when it identifies a reproducible property-outcome residual across public materials tasks that is not absorbed by composition, formula-size, target-family, split-leakage, or shuffled-target rivals.
domainSignificanceHypothesis.expectedDomainChange: Discovery-scored evidence would be a narrow computational-materials mechanism claim about public property outcome structure with independent holdout and replay support, not a matminer or pymatgen pipeline success.
domainSignificanceHypothesis.noveltyDiscriminator: The claimed signal must be not documented outside known source-family behavior and must change interpretation of the external target outcome. Candidate prediction: the descriptor-transfer property residual recurs across independent materials task slices after composition formula-size target-family split-leakage and shuffled-target controls. Rival prediction: composition formula-size target-family split leakage or shuffled-target controls erase the property residual.
domainSignificanceHypothesis.falsifier: The significance claim is falsified if source-family, metadata, simple baseline, or negative-control mechanisms erase the residual. composition formula-size target-family split leakage or shuffled-target controls erase the property residual

gates.message: HardSeed birth requires an explicit pre-birth domain-significance hypothesis, not only a runtime residual.
The domain-significance hypothesis must state the scientific/formal/measurement significance being tested.
The hypothesis must state what external domain interpretation, formal boundary, or measurement conclusion would change.
The hypothesis must define a novelty discriminator against known/source-family mechanisms.
The hypothesis must define a falsifier before HardSeed birth.
The domain-significance hypothesis must bind at least two public-safe external evidence refs already present in the runtime evidence set.
The domain-significance hypothesis must be tested by runtime evidence before HardSeed birth.
The domain-significance hypothesis must be supported by runtime evidence before HardSeed birth.

output.candidateMechanismPrediction: A descriptor-transfer perturbation can expose a materials property residual that recurs across public task slices after composition, formula-size, target-family, split-leakage, and shuffled-target controls. Prediction frozen for matbench_descriptor_transfer_significance_generator-target-05: the descriptor-transfer property residual recurs across independent materials task slices after composition formula-size target-family split-leakage and shuffled-target controls
output.rivalMechanismPrediction: Composition, formula size, element prevalence, task-family identity, split leakage, or shuffled-target controls explain the apparent property residual. Rival prediction for matbench_descriptor_transfer_significance_generator-target-05: composition formula-size target-family split leakage or shuffled-target controls erase the property residual

External problem anchor must cite a public-safe external source.
External problem anchor must define a measured target outcome.
External problem anchor must define the known baseline or prior rival state.

HardSeed birth requires an explicit pre-birth domain-significance hypothesis, not only a runtime residual.
The domain-significance hypothesis must state the scientific/formal/measurement significance being tested.
The hypothesis must state what external domain interpretation, formal boundary, or measurement conclusion would change.
The hypothesis must define a novelty discriminator against known/source-family mechanisms.
The hypothesis must define a falsifier before HardSeed birth.
The domain-significance hypothesis must bind at least two public-safe external evidence refs already present in the runtime evidence set.
The domain-significance hypothesis must be tested by runtime evidence before HardSeed birth.
The domain-significance hypothesis must be supported by runtime evidence before HardSeed birth.
HardSeed birth requires an external problem anchor with public inspectability and non-internal value before runtime evidence can count.
HardSeed birth requires a tested and supported domain-significance hypothesis before runtime evidence can count.
HardSeed birth requires loaded, executed, generated, or checked runtime evidence.
Deterministic design profiles are planning evidence only; HardSeed birth requires real loaded/executed/generated target evidence.
HardSeed birth requires public-safe source and evidence refs, including a concrete public URL.
HardSeed birth is blocked when any simple baseline explains the signal.
HardSeed birth is blocked when residual magnitude is below the second-stage pressure floor of 0.1.
HardSeed birth requires at least one rival mechanism to be weakened or scoped.
HardSeed birth requires a nontrivial residual beyond metadata, tool, pipeline, or baseline success.
HardSeed birth requires cross-source or cross-slice recurrence before InsightCandidate birth is possible.
HardSeed birth is blocked when counterexamples collapse the claim.
HardSeed birth requires an independent holdout/replay path or bounded nonfatal caveat.
